# MACRO REPORT — 2026-04-26
**Delivered:** 11:26 UTC | **Generated by:** Claude claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The dominant macro narrative heading into this week is the intersection of an active U.S.-Iran war, structurally elevated oil prices, and the most consequential Federal Reserve meeting in years — potentially Jerome Powell's final presiding meeting as Chair. Brent crude has surged roughly 12% over the past week amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions, pushing the IMF to warn of simultaneous inflation and recession risks globally [Source: https://www.icis.com/chemicals-and-the-economy/2026/04/imf-warns-on-inflation-and-recession-risks-as-iran-war-continues/]. The single most important variable for today's session is the **FOMC meeting outcome on April 28-29**, with markets near-certain the Fed holds at 3.50%–3.75%, but the forward guidance — particularly under incoming Chair nominee Kevin Warsh — will set the tone for the entire rate trajectory into H2 2026. The S&P 500 has managed record closes despite the conflict overhang, but the busiest earnings week of the season (five of the Magnificent 7 reporting) now creates a binary risk environment for equities [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/25/what-investors-should-watch-in-the-big-tech-earnings week-ahead/].

---

## 1. GLOBAL MACRO OVERVIEW

The U.S.-Iran conflict, now approximately eight weeks old, remains the primary macro shock reshaping global asset prices. The Strait of Hormuz has effectively been closed to normal oil and gas flows, driving visible global oil inventories down by an estimated 255 million barrels since the conflict began, according to Goldman Sachs data cited by ICIS — with inventories potentially reaching all-time lows even if the strait reopens by late April [Source: https://www.icis.com/chemicals-and-the-economy/2026/04/imf-warns-on-inflation-and-recession-risks-as-iran-war-continues/]. Brent crude settled near $99.13 per barrel as of April 24 [Source: Yahoo Finance (BZ=F)], though front-month contracts were trading above $107 during the week per the WSJ, with JPMorgan arguing oil prices have further to rise as demand destruction has not yet offset supply losses [Source: https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/JPMorgan-Says-Oil-Prices-Still-Have-Further-to-Rise.html].

The Federal Reserve is all but certain to hold the federal funds rate at 3.50%–3.75% at its April 28-29 meeting — the effective rate stood at 3.64% as of March [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS] — with 56 of 103 economists in a Reuters poll expecting no cut before September [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-23/fed-rate-cut-pushed-back-late-2026-war-related-inflation-risks-reuters]. The PCE inflation gauge is now projected to run at 3.7%, 3.4%, and 3.2% in Q2, Q3, and Q4 respectively, roughly 30 basis points above prior forecasts. U.S. CPI YoY stood at 3.32% as of March [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CPIAUCSL], and PPI MoM was running at 1.78% [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/PPIACO] — the worst U.S. inflation picture in almost four years according to MarketWatch [Source: https://www.marketwatch.com/story/u-s-inflation-picture-is-the-worst-in-almost-4-years-f3a3b03a].

The 10-year Treasury yield sat at 4.34% and the 2-year at 3.83% as of April 23 [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10 / https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS2], implying a mildly positive yield curve of ~51 basis points — a sign the market is pricing in eventual easing but not imminently. Gold and silver pulled back ahead of the FOMC, with gold trading in a range of $4,600–$4,900 per ounce in recent weeks per Kitco cited by Laodong.vn, under pressure from rising real yields and a higher-for-longer narrative [Source: https://www.idnfinancials.com/news/63245/gold-and-silver-prices-weaken-ahead-of-fomc-meeting].

On the equity front, the S&P 500 reached a record close during the week, finishing at 7,165.08 (+0.80%) on April 24 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GSPC)], while the Nasdaq 100 surged to 27,303.67 (+1.95%) on a strong Intel-led chip rally [Source: Yahoo Finance (^NDX)]. The VIX fell 3.11% to 18.71 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^VIX)], reflecting moderate but not extreme fear. The S&P 500's blended earnings growth rate for the current quarter stands at 15.1% YoY, above the 13.2% expectation at quarter-end, but five of the Magnificent 7 reporting this week creates significant event risk [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/25/what-investors-should-watch-in-the-big-tech-earnings-week-ahead/].

Global growth risks are mounting. The IMF has flagged simultaneous inflation and recession risks, and Egypt's growth forecasts have been trimmed due to war-driven energy price pressures [Source: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/egypt-economic-outlook-trimmed-slightly-due-iran-war-2026-04-26/]. Japan's core CPI accelerated to 1.8% in March, its first acceleration in five months, driven by energy prices — with analysts warning core inflation could approach 3% by fiscal year-end if crude stays elevated [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/24/japan-cpi-march-inflation-iran-war-boj-rate.html].

## 2. CONFLICT / GEOPOLITICAL STATUS

**U.S.-IRAN WAR — ACTIVE, WEEK 8**

- The U.S.-Iran conflict is now approximately eight weeks old, with the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed to normal oil and gas transit, causing a global energy supply shock. Goldman Sachs data shows visible global oil inventories have declined by 255 million barrels since the conflict began. [Source: https://www.icis.com/chemicals-and-the-economy/2026/04/imf-warns-on-inflation-and-recession-risks-as-iran-war-continues/]
- President Trump extended a two-week ceasefire with Iran, temporarily lifting equities to record highs on April 22, but lack of further progress has since weighed on sentiment. [Source: https://www.investopedia.com/stock-market-today-dow-jones-s-and-p-500-04232026-11956664]
- A second round of U.S.-Iran diplomatic talks is expected, with Iran's Foreign Minister reportedly en route to Pakistan as a possible intermediary. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/video/2026/04/24/final-march-consumer-sentiment-comes-in-at-49-point-8-above-estimates.html]
- Front-month Brent crude was trading above $107/bbl during the week, with JPMorgan arguing prices have further to rise as the market has not yet forced enough demand destruction to offset supply losses. [Source: https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/JPMorgan-Says-Oil-Prices-Still-Have-Further-to-Rise.html]
- Asia-U.S. container rates are stable to slightly higher, while tanker rates are mixed, reflecting ongoing shipping disruptions. [Source: https://www.icis.com/explore/resources/news/2026/04/24/11201519/asia-us-container-rates-stable-to-slightly-higher-tanker-rates-mixed]
- U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs saw 8 consecutive days of inflows totaling $2.1 billion, with BlackRock's IBIT leading — reflecting a partial risk-on rotation into alternative assets amid geopolitical uncertainty. [Source: https://decrypt.co/zh-Hans/news-explorer?pinned=1383522]
- The IMF has formally warned on simultaneous inflation and recession risks tied to the Iran conflict's impact on global energy markets. [Source: https://www.icis.com/chemicals-and-the-economy/2026/04/imf-warns-on-inflation-and-recession-risks-as-iran-war-continues/]

## 5. GLOBAL EQUITY INDICES

### US Equities

**S&P 500 — 7,165.08 (+0.80%)** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
The S&P 500 closed at fresh records during the week at 7,165.08 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GSPC)], with blended Q1 earnings growth running at 15.1% YoY — ahead of the 13.2% expectation at quarter-end [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/25/what-investors-should-watch-in-the-big-tech-earnings-week-ahead/]. However, the index remains sensitive to oil-driven inflation shock. HSBC's Max Kettner noted investors "don't want to be underweight stocks prior to megacap earnings" [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/24/procter-gamble-pg-q3-2026-earnings.html].

**Nasdaq 100 — 27,303.67 (+1.95%)** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
The Nasdaq 100 surged 1.95% to 27,303.67 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^NDX)], led by an Intel chip rally. The index set fresh record closes and now faces a critical test this week with Alphabet, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft (Wednesday after close) and Apple (Thursday after close) all reporting [Source: https://www.investors.com/market-trend/stock-market-today/dow-jones-sp500-nasdaq-intel-stock-intc-earnings-amd-arm/].

**Dow Jones — 49,230.71 (-0.16%)** | Bias: Neutral
The Dow Jones underperformed at 49,230.71 (-0.16%) [Source: Yahoo Finance (^DJI)], weighed by old-economy names more exposed to energy cost inflation. P&G beat estimates with Q3 EPS of $1.63 adjusted vs. estimates, with revenue of $21.24 billion, and reiterated full-year guidance — shares rose more than 2% in premarket [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/24/procter-gamble-pg-q3-2026-earnings.html].

### Asia

**Nikkei 225 — 59,716.18 (+0.97%)** | Bias: Neutral
The Nikkei rose 0.97% to 59,716.18 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^N225)], despite Japan's accelerating inflation environment. Core CPI hit 1.8% in March, its first acceleration in five months, driven by Iran war energy price effects [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/24/japan-cpi-march-inflation-iran-war-boj-rate.html]. BoJ is under growing pressure to signal policy normalisation.

**KOSPI — 6,475.63 (flat)** | Bias: Neutral
The KOSPI was virtually unchanged at 6,475.63 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^KS11)], reflecting South Korea's balanced exposure as both an energy importer (negative from Brent) and tech exporter (positive from global chip demand). The index awaits clearer macro direction from the FOMC and Mag 7 earnings.

**ASX 200 — 8,786.50 (-0.08%)** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
The ASX 200 was marginally lower at 8,786.50 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^AXJO)], despite commodity price tailwinds for Australia's resource-heavy index. The RBA's rate path and China demand trajectory remain key variables for direction.

### Europe

**DAX — 24,128.98 (-0.11%)** | Bias: Neutral
The DAX was marginally lower at 24,128.98 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GDAXI)], with European markets caught between strong Q1 corporate earnings (Italy's public markets hit all-time highs pre-conflict per PitchBook) and growing geopolitical headwinds from the Iran war's energy impact on European industry [Source: https://pitchbook.com/news/reports/q1-2026-italy-market-snapshot]. ECB is navigating a stagflationary impulse with limited policy flexibility.

## 8. WEEK AHEAD / SCENARIO ANALYSIS

| SCENARIO | PROBABILITY | TRIGGER | BRENT | S&P 500 | BTC |
|----------|-------------|---------|-------|---------|-----|
| Bull | 25% | Iran ceasefire breakthrough + Mag 7 earnings beat + FOMC dovish tilt from Warsh | $88 | 7,450 | $88,000 |
| Base | 55% | FOMC holds at 3.50-3.75%, cautious Warsh tone; Mag 7 mixed; Iran stalemate persists | $97 | 7,200 | $79,000 |
| Bear | 20% | Iran conflict escalates (Hormuz remains blocked), Mag 7 misses on margins, Warsh hawkish surprise | $112 | 6,850 | $68,000 |

### Key Upcoming Catalysts for the Week

1. **April 28-29 — FOMC Meeting Decision:** Near-certain hold at 3.50%–3.75%, but the critical variable is forward guidance. This may be Jerome Powell's final meeting as Chair; Kevin Warsh's confirmation trajectory will dominate forward policy narrative. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/24/fed-likely-to-hold-rates-steady-april-consumer-impact.html]

2. **April 30 (Wednesday, after close) — Mag 7 Earnings (Alphabet, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft):** The single largest earnings event of the quarter. S&P 500 blended earnings growth at 15.1% YoY; any guidance cuts citing Iran war cost pressures could trigger a significant de-rating. [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/25/what-investors-should-watch-in-the-big-tech-earnings-week-ahead/]

3. **May 1 (Thursday, after close) — Apple Earnings:** Apple's consumer demand signals amid the Iran war's inflation drag on discretionary spending will be closely watched. Consumer sentiment final reading came in at 49.8 in March, near multi-year lows. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/video/2026/04/24/final-march-consumer-sentiment-comes-in-at-49-point-8-above-estimates.html]

4. **Ongoing — Iran War Diplomatic Developments:** Second round of U.S.-Iran talks reportedly imminent via Pakistan intermediary. Any ceasefire extension or de-escalation signal would represent the most impactful positive catalyst for global risk assets and would drive Brent sharply lower. [Source: https://www.icis.com/chemicals-and-the-economy/2026/04/imf-warns-on-inflation-and-recession-risks-as-iran-war-continues/]

5. **Week of April 28 — U.S. PCE Inflation Data (April 30):** The Fed's preferred inflation gauge is forecast to run at 3.7% in Q2 annually. A hot print would confirm the higher-for-longer narrative, pressuring Treasuries and gold while strengthening the dollar. [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-23/fed-rate-cut-pushed-back-late-2026-war-related-inflation-risks-reuters]

---
